data.title: Bitmine’s NYSE Uplist Puts Ethereum Staking Model In Investor Focus
data.description:
• Bitmine Immersion Technologies (NYSEAM:BMNR) plans to uplist its shares from NYSE American to the New York Stock Exchange on April 9, 2026.
  • The company reports holding more than 4.8 million ETH and staking over 3.3 million ETH through its MAVAN platform.
  • These moves position Bitmine as the largest single Ethereum staking entity and a key infrastructure provider in the digital asset space.

For investors watching the intersection of public equities and digital assets, Bitmine Immersion Technologies is becoming closely tied to Ethereum. The company has leaned into an ETH centered approach, with large token holdings and extensive staking activity through its MAVAN platform. This positions its business profile squarely around Ethereum infrastructure and staking economics rather than traditional mining alone.

With the planned NYSE uplist and sizable on chain footprint, BMNR is stepping into a much wider spotlight. Investors considering the stock now need to think about both exchange visibility and the implications of being tethered to one of the largest capital pools in the Ethereum ecosystem.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Bitmine Immersion Technologies by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Bitmine Immersion Technologies.

1 thing going right for Bitmine Immersion Technologies that this headline doesn't cover.

Bitmine’s NYSE uplist and MAVAN launch pull two powerful themes together for investors. On one side, the move to the New York Stock Exchange typically brings higher visibility with global institutions that may prefer large, liquid venues. On the other, MAVAN shifts Bitmine from mainly holding Ethereum to actively monetizing it through staking at scale. With 4,803,334 ETH in treasury, about 3.98% of Ethereum’s supply, and more than 3.3 million ETH already staked, the business is leaning heavily into Ethereum economics and fee-like staking income. Reported annualized staking revenue in the US$196 million to roughly US$300 million range, against US$11.4b of crypto, cash, and “moonshot” holdings, shows how central this funding engine has become. For you as an investor, the key question is whether this combination of exchange visibility and Ethereum concentration justifies treating BMNR less like a software stock and more like an ETH-backed infrastructure play with treasury management at its core.

Advertisement

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Heavy concentration in Ethereum and other digital assets means BMNR’s balance sheet and earnings are tightly linked to crypto price swings.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged 2 important risks, including substantial shareholder dilution over the past year and a volatile share price over 3 months compared with the US market.
  • 🎁 MAVAN turns Bitmine’s large ETH position into a yield-generating platform, with annualized staking rewards reported in the hundreds of millions of US dollars.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow 86.84% per year. If realized, this would support the case that MAVAN and the Ethereum-centric model can scale financially.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on a few practical markers. First, track how quickly MAVAN attracts outside institutional clients beyond Bitmine’s own ETH, because that will show whether the platform can become a third party infrastructure business rather than just an internal tool. Second, watch the proportion of total ETH that is staked versus held idle, along with any updates to expected annual staking rewards, to gauge how efficiently Bitmine is using its treasury. Third, keep an eye on further equity issuance or leverage, given the past shareholder dilution and large crypto exposure. Finally, monitor how the NYSE uplist affects liquidity and institutional ownership, as that can influence how closely BMNR trades in line with Ethereum or starts to reflect its own fundamentals more distinctly.

• If you are wondering whether Venture Global at around US$15.99 is still offering value after a strong run, this breakdown is for you.
  • The stock has delivered returns of 1.5% over 7 days, 28.1% over 30 days, 127.1% year to date and 105.8% over the last year, which naturally raises questions about how much upside or risk might now be priced in.
  • Recent coverage around Venture Global has focused on its position within the energy sector and how investors are reacting to its share price performance. This context matters because sentiment shifts can influence whether the current price reflects fundamentals or more short term enthusiasm.
  • Venture Global currently has a valuation score of 3/6. The rest of this article will walk through what that means using several common valuation approaches before finishing with a broader way to think about what "fair value" could look like for this stock.

Venture Global delivered 105.8% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Oil and Gas industry.

Approach 1: Venture Global Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For Venture Global, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $11,614.68 million. Analyst and extrapolated estimates in the ten year forecast show free cash flow moving from losses in the nearer years to a projected $3,105.50 million in 2030, with interim figures such as $2,375.08 million in 2029 and $3,670.69 million in 2031.

Putting all those discounted cash flows together, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of $28.66 per share. At a recent share price of about $15.99, this implies a 44.2% discount, which indicates Venture Global is trading below this DCF estimate of fair value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Venture Global is undervalued by 44.2%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 61 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Venture Global.

Approach 2: Venture Global Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful check on what you are paying for each dollar of earnings, because it directly links the share price to the business’s current profit generation.

In simple terms, higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can justify a higher “normal” P/E ratio, while slower expected growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower one. So context really matters when you compare P/E numbers.

Venture Global currently trades on a P/E of 17.38x. That sits above the Oil and Gas industry average of 15.66x, but below the peer group average of 32.13x. Simply Wall St’s “Fair Ratio” for Venture Global is 13.18x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might reasonably be, given the company’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, size, risk factors and its industry.

The Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple industry or peer comparison because it adjusts for company specific traits rather than assuming all Oil and Gas stocks deserve the same multiple.

Since Venture Global’s actual P/E of 17.38x is above the Fair Ratio of 13.18x by more than a small margin, the shares screen as expensive on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so Narratives are introduced here as a simple way for you to attach a clear story about Venture Global to the numbers you see. You can link your view on its future revenue, earnings and margins to a financial forecast and then to a fair value estimate that can be compared with today’s price on Simply Wall St’s Community page. Narratives are updated automatically when fresh news or earnings arrive, and one investor might build a bullish Venture Global Narrative around a US$18.70 fair value while another might build a more cautious one around US$9.00. This gives you a structured, easy to use framework for deciding whether the current US$15.99 share price looks high or low relative to the story you believe.

For Venture Global however we'll make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Venture Global Narratives:

🐂 Venture Global Bull Case

Fair value used in this bullish narrative: US$18.70

Implied discount to this fair value at US$15.99: about 14.5% undervalued

Revenue growth assumption: 23.12% a year

  • Assumes Venture Global scales LNG capacity toward more than 100 MTPA, using modular technology and data driven operations to keep costs low while pursuing long term contracts and additional uncontracted volumes.
  • Builds on bullish analyst expectations for revenue growing at about 23.1% annually, earnings of roughly US$2.2b by 2028 and a higher future P/E of 27.9x compared with the current industry level cited in the narrative.
  • Recognises risks around arbitration outcomes, LNG spread compression, execution on megaprojects and heavy use of debt, and values the shares at a fair value of US$18.70 based on those optimistic assumptions and a 9.41% discount rate.

🐻 Venture Global Bear Case

Fair value used in this bearish narrative: US$9.00

Implied premium to this fair value at US$15.99: about 77.7% overvalued

Revenue growth assumption: 12.67% a year

  • Starts from the view that arbitration uncertainty, capital intensive expansion projects and potential moderation in LNG pricing could weigh on free cash flow and margins even as revenues grow.
  • Uses bearish analyst assumptions of revenue growing at about 12.7% annually, profit margins falling sharply toward 0.5% and earnings of about US$72.0m by 2028, paired with a very high implied future P/E multiple of around 412x.
  • Highlights the possibility that greater use of short term contracts, project delays, regulatory shifts and geopolitical risks could limit long term contract signings and supports a fair value around US$9.00 under a 9.07% discount rate.

data.title: Lego CFO Joins PVH Board As Earnings Quality Takes Center Stage
data.description:
• PVH Corp (NYSE:PVH) has appointed Jesper Andersen, CFO of the Lego Group, to its Board of Directors.
  • The appointment brings financial and operational experience from a major global consumer brand to PVH's board.

PVH, the owner of Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, operates as a global apparel and lifestyle company with a focus on building its brands worldwide. The addition of Andersen comes at a time when many consumer companies are paying close attention to brand strength, digital engagement, and supply chain efficiency. For you as an investor, board composition can be one of the clearer signals about how a company wants to position itself for these industry priorities.

Andersen's background in finance and operations at Lego, a well known global brand, may shape how PVH approaches capital allocation, digital initiatives, and brand building. Investors tracking NYSE:PVH may view this board move as part of a broader effort to support the company's ambitions for Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger as global lifestyle brands, and may watch future disclosures and decisions for indications of how this influence develops.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for PVH by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on PVH.

Does the team leading PVH have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

For PVH, bringing in Jesper Andersen as a director and audit and risk committee member looks closely tied to the current phase of its story. The company has reported full year sales of US$8.95b and net income of US$25.3m, with results affected by a US$479.5m one off loss and tariff pressures. At the same time, management is pushing the PVH+ roadmap, cost savings, and a shift toward direct to consumer sales while having already deployed US$3,735.66m into buybacks over several years. A board member who is a sitting CFO at Lego, a large global consumer brand with complex supply chains and digital distribution, may influence how PVH thinks about capital discipline, risk oversight and execution of brand investments at Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger. For you as an investor, the key angle is not only the headline appointment but also whether future decisions on marketing spend, tariff mitigation, impairments and further capital returns reflect tighter alignment between PVH’s financial profile and its brand driven growth ambitions.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The PVH Narrative

  • Andersen’s experience with global expansion, consumer engagement and digital sales at Lego aligns with the PVH+ focus on international growth, direct to consumer channels and operational simplification highlighted in the community narrative.
  • The narrative leans heavily on margin improvement and smoother execution, while PVH has recently reported a sharp drop in net income and a one off impairment; a risk focused director may push for more conservative assumptions around profitability and capital allocation.
  • The appointment directly into the audit and risk committee brings an additional lens on tariff exposure, large non cash charges and buyback history, elements that are mentioned in the narrative but may not fully reflect how a new director could influence future risk appetite.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for PVH to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Profitability has been thin, with full year net income of US$25.3m on US$8.95b of sales and a recent US$479.5m one off loss, so investors need to watch whether the board can strengthen earnings quality.
  • ⚠️ Tariff headwinds and margin pressure have already been flagged, and a cautious 2026 outlook with potential earnings impact means execution missteps could weigh on returns even with board level financial expertise.
  • 🎁 Analysts highlight earnings growth potential and views that PVH trades below some estimates of fair value, so stronger governance and risk oversight from a seasoned CFO could support efforts to rebuild margins.
  • 🎁 PVH has already completed a substantial share buyback over several years and continues to focus on Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, giving the new director a sizeable capital allocation and brand platform to influence.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on how Andersen’s arrival shows up in PVH’s decisions rather than treating the appointment as a one off headline. Keep an eye on future earnings calls for changes in how management discusses tariff mitigation, impairments and marketing spend for Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, and whether the audit and risk committee commentary becomes more prominent. It is also worth tracking how capital allocation evolves, including any updates on buybacks or balance sheet priorities, especially given recent swings in reported earnings. For context, compare PVH’s progress and messaging with other global brand owners such as Ralph Lauren, Tapestry or Capri Holdings to see whether board level changes translate into clearer financial discipline.

data.title: Earnings Update: Tosei Corporation Beat Earnings And Now Analysts Have New Forecasts For This Year
data.description:
A week ago, Tosei Corporation (TSE:8923) came out with a strong set of first-quarter numbers that could potentially lead to a re-rate of the stock. Performance was better than the analysts expected, with revenues of JP¥60b coming in22% ahead of expectations, and statutory earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥105 exceeding forecasts by 20%. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. So we gathered the latest post-earnings forecasts to see what estimates suggest is in store for next year.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Following the latest results, Tosei's four analysts are now forecasting revenues of JP¥119.4b in 2026. This would be a solid 9.4% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are forecast to reduce 4.7% to JP¥165 in the same period. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥119.1b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥163 in 2026. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

View our latest analysis for Tosei

The consensus price target rose 11% to JP¥1,998despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of Tosei's earnings by assigning a price premium. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. Currently, the most bullish analyst values Tosei at JP¥2,270 per share, while the most bearish prices it at JP¥1,725. With such a narrow range of valuations, the analysts apparently share similar views on what they think the business is worth.

These estimates are interesting, but it can be useful to paint some more broad strokes when seeing how forecasts compare, both to the Tosei's past performance and to peers in the same industry. The period to the end of 2026 brings more of the same, according to the analysts, with revenue forecast to display 13% growth on an annualised basis. That is in line with its 11% annual growth over the past five years. Compare this with the broader industry, which analyst estimates (in aggregate) suggest will see revenues grow 4.5% annually. So it's pretty clear that Tosei is forecast to grow substantially faster than its industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that there's been no major change in sentiment, with the analysts reconfirming that the business is performing in line with their previous earnings per share estimates. Fortunately, they also reconfirmed their revenue numbers, suggesting that it's tracking in line with expectations. Additionally, our data suggests that revenue is expected to grow faster than the wider industry. There was also a nice increase in the price target, with the analysts clearly feeling that the intrinsic value of the business is improving.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. At Simply Wall St, we have a full range of analyst estimates for Tosei going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here..

data.title: Here's Why We're Watching Aurora Labs' (ASX:A3D) Cash Burn Situation
data.description:
We can readily understand why investors are attracted to unprofitable companies. For example, biotech and mining exploration companies often lose money for years before finding success with a new treatment or mineral discovery. But the harsh reality is that very many loss making companies burn through all their cash and go bankrupt.

Given this risk, we thought we'd take a look at whether Aurora Labs (ASX:A3D) shareholders should be worried about its cash burn. In this report, we will consider the company's annual negative free cash flow, henceforth referring to it as the 'cash burn'. We'll start by comparing its cash burn with its cash reserves in order to calculate its cash runway.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

How Long Is Aurora Labs' Cash Runway?

A company's cash runway is calculated by dividing its cash hoard by its cash burn. As at December 2025, Aurora Labs had cash of AU$3.6m and such minimal debt that we can ignore it for the purposes of this analysis. In the last year, its cash burn was AU$4.2m. Therefore, from December 2025 it had roughly 10 months of cash runway. To be frank, this kind of short runway puts us on edge, as it indicates the company must reduce its cash burn significantly, or else raise cash imminently. The image below shows how its cash balance has been changing over the last few years.

View our latest analysis for Aurora Labs

How Is Aurora Labs' Cash Burn Changing Over Time?

In our view, Aurora Labs doesn't yet produce significant amounts of operating revenue, since it reported just AU$708k in the last twelve months. As a result, we think it's a bit early to focus on the revenue growth, so we'll limit ourselves to looking at how the cash burn is changing over time. Over the last year its cash burn actually increased by a very significant 65%. While this spending increase is no doubt intended to drive growth, if the trend continues the company's cash runway will shrink very quickly. Admittedly, we're a bit cautious of Aurora Labs due to its lack of significant operating revenues. So we'd generally prefer stocks from this list of stocks that have analysts forecasting growth.

Can Aurora Labs Raise More Cash Easily?

Since its cash burn is moving in the wrong direction, Aurora Labs shareholders may wish to think ahead to when the company may need to raise more cash. Generally speaking, a listed business can raise new cash through issuing shares or taking on debt. Commonly, a business will sell new shares in itself to raise cash and drive growth. We can compare a company's cash burn to its market capitalisation to get a sense for how many new shares a company would have to issue to fund one year's operations.

Since it has a market capitalisation of AU$29m, Aurora Labs' AU$4.2m in cash burn equates to about 15% of its market value. Given that situation, it's fair to say the company wouldn't have much trouble raising more cash for growth, but shareholders would be somewhat diluted.

Is Aurora Labs' Cash Burn A Worry?

Even though its increasing cash burn makes us a little nervous, we are compelled to mention that we thought Aurora Labs' cash burn relative to its market cap was relatively promising. Looking at the factors mentioned in this short report, we do think that its cash burn is a bit risky, and it does make us slightly nervous about the stock. On another note, Aurora Labs has 4 warning signs (and 3 which make us uncomfortable) we think you should know about.

data.title: and ST HD Co.,Ltd. Just Missed EPS By 18%: Here's What Analysts Think Will Happen Next
data.description:
As you might know, and ST HD Co.,Ltd. (TSE:2685) recently reported its yearly numbers. Revenues were in line with forecasts, at JP¥304b, although statutory earnings per share came in 18% below what the analysts expected, at JP¥206 per share. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. We thought readers would find it interesting to see the analysts latest (statutory) post-earnings forecasts for next year.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Taking into account the latest results, the consensus forecast from and ST HDLtd's six analysts is for revenues of JP¥315.3b in 2027. This reflects an okay 3.6% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are predicted to grow 14% to JP¥235. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥313.6b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥241 in 2027. So it looks like there's been a small decline in overall sentiment after the recent results - there's been no major change to revenue estimates, but the analysts did make a small dip in their earnings per share forecasts.

View our latest analysis for and ST HDLtd

It might be a surprise to learn that the consensus price target was broadly unchanged at JP¥3,060, with the analysts clearly implying that the forecast decline in earnings is not expected to have much of an impact on valuation. That's not the only conclusion we can draw from this data however, as some investors also like to consider the spread in estimates when evaluating analyst price targets. The most optimistic and ST HDLtd analyst has a price target of JP¥3,300 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at JP¥2,900. Even so, with a relatively close grouping of estimates, it looks like the analysts are quite confident in their valuations, suggesting and ST HDLtd is an easy business to forecast or the the analysts are all using similar assumptions.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. We would highlight that and ST HDLtd's revenue growth is expected to slow, with the forecast 3.6% annualised growth rate until the end of 2027 being well below the historical 10% p.a. growth over the last five years. Compare this against other companies (with analyst forecasts) in the industry, which are in aggregate expected to see revenue growth of 7.4% annually. So it's pretty clear that, while revenue growth is expected to slow down, the wider industry is also expected to grow faster than and ST HDLtd.

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analysts downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. Fortunately, the analysts also reconfirmed their revenue estimates, suggesting that it's tracking in line with expectations. Although our data does suggest that and ST HDLtd's revenue is expected to perform worse than the wider industry. There was no real change to the consensus price target, suggesting that the intrinsic value of the business has not undergone any major changes with the latest estimates.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have estimates - from multiple and ST HDLtd analysts - going out to 2029, and you can see them free on our platform here.
